Figurative Meaning: A person who remains calm and composed during conflict or pressure
Non-Idiomatic Equivalent: Mutum mai natsuwa a lokacin matsala
Cultural Origin or Historical Reference: Water imagery reflects Lake Chad basin culture where calm water symbolizes wisdom
First Example Sentence: Ali kannu ruwa-ro goro, ko da mutane suna gardama.
Second Example Sentence: Malaminmu kannu ruwa-ro goro lokacin rikici a aji.
Third Example Sentence: Shugaban kauye kannu ruwa-ro goro lokacin taro.

Figurative Meaning: To be extremely hungry or desperate
Cultural Origin or Historical Reference: Food scarcity historically shaped expressions around hunger in Sahel communities
First Example Sentence: Ngəna yimi kulu-ro kura bayan tafiya mai tsawo.
Second Example Sentence: Yaro yimi kulu-ro kura bayan wasa.
Third Example Sentence: Dalibai yimi kulu-ro kura bayan darasi.

Cultural Origin or Historical Reference: Linked to traditional council deliberations in Kanuri governance

Second Example Sentence: Jagora kannu kura-ngə lawan kafin hukunci.
Third Example Sentence: Dan kasuwa kannu kura-ngə lawan kafin ciniki.

Figurative Meaning: A situation that quickly becomes chaotic

Second Example Sentence: Kasuwar ta zama ruwa kadi-ngə kulu lokacin rikici.
Third Example Sentence: Aji ya zama ruwa kadi-ngə kulu lokacin da malam ya fita.

Third Example Sentence: Malami kannu yime-ro tashi lokacin hayaniya.

Second Example Sentence: Ali kulu kura-ro yimi domin gina gida.
Third Example Sentence: Dalibi kulu kura-ro yimi kafin jarabawa.
